properties.remark: QUARTER SIZED HAIL ALONG WITH ESTIMATED GUSTS OF UP TO 65MPH NEAR FORNEY CREEK AT BACKCOUNTRY CAMPSITE 68
A FEW LARGE LIMBS DOWN AT A RESIDENCE ALONG WEST SPRINGS HIGHWAY. HE ALSO HAD PEA SIZED HAIL.
HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A TREE IN THE ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF MORRIS FARM ROAD AND WHITLOCK LAKE ROAD.
TREES DOWN ON OLD PUMP STATION ROAD. ALSO A REPORT OF HAIL ON NEARBY VAUGHAN ROAD BUT NO SIZE OF THE HAIL WAS GIVEN.

HAIL BEGAN TO FALL AROUND 7 PM AND CONTINUED AT LEAST TO 710 PM COVERING THE GROUND. ODD SHAPED HAIL WITH POINTS AND SOME LOOKING LIKE STARS. HAIL RANGED UP TO AS LARGE AS GOLF BALL SIZED IN LENGTH OR DIAMETER. SPOTTER ALSO HAD A STRONG WIND GUST THAT BLEW AROUND LIGHT OBJECTS IN HIS YARD.
AT LEAST TWO TREES DOWN IN THE AREA. ONE BLOCKING WHITNEY ROAD AND THE OTHER DOWN ALONG BERRY FARM ROAD. THESE TREES ARE DOWN ROUGHLY TWO MILES APART.
A LARGE PINE TREE WAS ALSO BLOWN DOWN AT STEEL CREEK PARK.

A CAMPGROUND ALONG BUNT CREEK IN THE PINEOLA CAMPING AREA HAS BEEN EVACUATED DUE TO APPROACHING WATER.A BRIDGE ON GREENE ROAD IS UNDER FOUR FEET OF WATER ASSOCIATED WITH THE LINVILLE RIVER. A SINKHOLE HAS BEEN REPORTED IN NEWLAND ON TEMPLE SRINGS ROAD.
NUMEROUS REPORTS OF FLOODING ACROSS THE CITY OF CHARLOTTE. APARTMENTS FLOODED ON WALLACE DR...AND TACOMA ST.
THE STEAM GAUGE ON BIG DUTCHMAN CREEK HAS RISEN ABOVE FLOOD STAGE TO A LEVEL OF 9.76 FEET.
THE STREAM GAUGE AT LINCREST PLACE ON MULLEN CREEK HAS RISEN ABOVE FLOOD STAGE TO A LEVEL OF 8.41 FEET.
THE STREAM GAUGE ON WILDCAT CREEK BELOW ROCK HILL HAS RISEN ABOVE FLOOD STAGE TO A LEVEL OF 9.33 FEET.
THE STREAM GAUGE AT LINCREST PLACE ON MULLEN CREEK HAS RISEN ABOVE FLOOD STAGE TO A LEVEL OF 8.41 FEET.
THE STREAM GAUGE ON MALLARD CREEK AT HARRISBURG REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 13 FEET. AT THIS STAGE...ROADS AND AMPHI-THEATER PARKING LOT NEAR THE GREENWAY WILL BE FLOODED.

COUNTY ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED AT THE LAKE JUNALUSKA DAM DUE TO EXCESSIVE FLOW FROM THE LAKE. ALSO VEHICLES HAD BECOME BRIEFLY STRANDED AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 19 AND 276 IN DELLWOOD AS THEY STALLED OUT IN HIGH WATER.
